channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish dumped in car park, 10th July
item.description:
Bin bag load full of rubbish has been dumped in the car park in between dighton court and Hillingdon street. It’s already attracting lots of flies and is very smelly

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): John Ruskin Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trash, 10th July
item.description:
Underpass full of trash, people using drugs and urine smell

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pettman Crescent, Thamesmead

item.title: Flat pack sheets walk way fm Briset Rd to Rochester Way, 10th July
item.description:
Flat pack sheets, walk way fm Briset Rd to Rochester Way. Just past Foster Rd

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Briset Road, Eltham

item.title: Bin dumped on pavement, 10th July
item.description:
Reported this bin on 22 June, now looks like people are dumping poo bags and this bin has not moved in weeks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Davis Way, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing street bin next to bus stop, 10th July
item.description:
Overflowing bin covered in flies on pavement next to busy bus stop.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Northwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No team on roadworks, 10th July
item.description:
From the attached photos, the map shows Southmere Drive. This is the main entrance / exit for residents and the school nearby. It is very busy. The cones have been put across so that residents cannot turn off Yarnton Way into Southmere Drive and have to continue up Yarnton Way and double back. Yarnton Way is an extremely busy road and blocking any part of it causes traffic to build up, especially during the school run as we have several schools nearby. On the opposite side, there is legal parking and the cones have decreased the road width so that if a car is parked on the end of the parking run, it is very difficult to navigate past it as there is now little room either side. The vans and buses struggle to get through so that they do not damage other vehiclees. This work was due to be done between 6 - 10 July - obviously, this always depends on circumstances etc. However, since the cones were put out on Sunday 5th, no team / workforce has been near nor by and no work at all has been conducted. Due to the traffic in the area and the main route in and out of Southmere Estate, could you please chase this up so that work is done and the road is cleared of cones.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Yarnton Way, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping is a chronic problem in this area of Trinity Place. Ever since the new flats were built, residents that have moved in or out of the flats have dumped their unwanted furniture and fixtures on the pavements and side road., 10th July
item.description:
This incident of fly tipping was witnessed by a resident of Trinity Place, it occurred on Wednesday 8th July 2026, and the person responsible was/is a resident of Albery Apartments (19 Trinity Place). This isn't the first time this has occurred.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11;13, Trinity Place, Bexley, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegal parking in Ashwin Street, 10th July
item.description:
This car has been parked here for over an hour. Blocking the gate, on a double hello and blocking the drive through.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ashwin Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 10th July
item.description:
I saw a van drop off this car. I specifically saw him because I had to stop him from urinating near my front door. He dumped the car off his low loader and put a note on it saying ‘just broken down’ and removed the number plates. This car clearly hasn’t just broken down. There also a bit of wood behind the wheels to maybe stop it from moving?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 79, Gipsy Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A large branch has broken from the tree and is precariously attached ., 10th July
item.description:
It wouldn’t take some kids much to pull in the broken branch which is very big and very heavy Would hate to think of the accident this could cause 2 nd to last tree in St Mary’s

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lesley Close, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian Safety, 10th July
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-2, Blackfen Road, Blackfen, Greater London

item.title: Broken bin and glass by school, 10th July
item.description:
Hello The only bin on Penshurst Road (it requires at least one more that’s why there’s litter everywhere). Is broken and needs fixing. There is also glass on the floor here just by the school.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Penshurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
